text: California personal injury primer: Most PI claims arise from negligence — the defendant owed a duty, breached it, and caused compensable harm. Auto collisions, slip-and-fall, dog bites, and premises liability are common. California is a pure comparative negligence state: a plaintiff can recover even if partially at fault, with damages reduced by their percentage of fault. Economic damages include medical bills, lost wages, and property damage; non-economic damages cover pain and suffering. Insurance minimums are low — many serious injuries exceed policy limits, making underinsured motorist coverage relevant.

California PI evidence basics: Police reports are influential but not conclusive — fault may be listed as undetermined when officers lack witnesses. Medical records from ER visits, imaging orders, and follow-up care establish injury severity. Document everything in the first 72 hours: photos of vehicles, scene, injuries; witness names; and the other driver's insurance information. Comparative negligence analysis weighs traffic signals, right-of-way rules, and whether any party violated California Vehicle Code sections cited on the report.

California statute of limitations for personal injury: Generally two years from the date of injury for negligence claims (CCP §335.1). Government entity claims require a notice of claim within six months, then suit within six months after claim denial or two years if no response. Discovery rule may toll the period when injury was not immediately discoverable. Minors toll until age 18 for most claims. Always verify accident date against today's date before qualifying.

Texas statute of limitations for personal injury: Two years from the date the cause of action accrues for most negligence claims (CPRC §16.003). Government claims have shorter notice requirements. Medical malpractice has special rules. Compare accident date to today — cases outside the window are generally not viable for referral.

Florida statute of limitations for personal injury: Four years from the date of injury for negligence claims. Wrongful death is two years. Government claims require notice within three years and suit within three years after notice. Verify accident date carefully for Florida callers.

Comparative negligence rules: California uses pure comparative fault — recovery is reduced by plaintiff's fault percentage but never barred. Texas uses modified comparative (51% bar — plaintiff cannot recover if more than 50% at fault). Florida uses pure comparative negligence. When a caller's verbal account conflicts with a police report listing fault as undetermined, do not accuse — ask a gentle clarifying question about what they personally observed versus what the report states.

First 72 hours after an accident — plaintiff checklist: Seek medical care even if you feel fine (delayed onset is common). Call police and obtain a report number. Photograph vehicles, scene, road conditions, and visible injuries. Exchange insurance and contact information; do not admit fault at the scene. Notify your own insurer. Preserve damaged property. Avoid posting about the accident on social media. Contact a PI attorney before giving recorded statements to adverse insurers.

Martinez & Associates — Orange County, California. Specialties: auto accidents, rear-end collisions, uninsured motorist claims. Languages: English and Spanish; bilingual intake staff and attorneys. Typical case profile: MVA with documented treatment, clear or disputed liability in OC/LA corridor. Phone: (714) 555-0142. Accepts moderate to high-value auto cases. Strong Spanish-language client communication.

Brennan Law — Los Angeles County, California. Specialties: motorcycle accidents, auto collisions, catastrophic injury. Languages: English only. Phone: (310) 555-0198. Prefers cases with hospitalization or surgery. Does not handle slip-and-fall routinely.

Reyes Injury Law — San Diego County, California. Specialties: slip-and-fall, premises liability, auto accidents. Languages: English and Spanish (bilingual). Phone: (619) 555-0167. Strong in commercial property and grocery-store fall cases.

Patel Personal Injury — San Francisco Bay Area, California. Specialties: all PI types — auto, pedestrian, rideshare, product liability. Languages: English, Spanish, Hindi. Phone: (415) 555-0133. High volume; accepts wide range of case values.

Cohen Law Group — statewide California. Specialties: high-value PI only — cases with expected value $100K+. Languages: English only. Phone: (800) 555-0171. Requires hospitalization, surgery, or permanent injury documentation. Declines soft-tissue-only rear-end cases.

Comparable settlement — California rear-end MVA, moderate soft tissue: Female driver rear-ended at stoplight in Anaheim, CA. Whiplash, 8 weeks PT, ER visit, no surgery. Liability admitted. Settlement range $32,000–$48,000. Economic damages ~$12K medical; 2x multiplier on specials.

Comparable settlement — California rear-end MVA, disputed liability: Male driver rear-ended on I-5 in Orange County. Police report fault undetermined; other driver claimed right of way. MRI-confirmed disc bulge, ongoing PT. Settlement range $55,000–$80,000 after litigation threat. Liability split roughly 70/30.

Comparable settlement — California rear-end, Spanish-speaking plaintiff, OC: Rear collision June 2025, whiplash and headaches, ER discharge with MRI ordered. Fault disputed on police report. Comparable cases in Orange County settled $45,000–$72,000 with documented imaging and treatment compliance.

Comparable settlement — Texas grocery store slip-and-fall: Customer slipped on unmarked wet floor in Houston. Fractured wrist, surgery, 3 months lost work. Premises liability clear via store video. Settlement range $85,000–$120,000.
Comparable settlement — Texas slip-and-fall, moderate: Ankle sprain and back strain, 6 weeks treatment, no surgery. Liability disputed. Settlement range $40,000–$65,000 in Dallas County.

Comparable settlement — California motorcycle collision: Rider struck by left-turning vehicle in LA. Fractured tibia, 5-day hospitalization, 6 months PT. Liability 80% on turning driver. Settlement range $145,000–$200,000.

Comparable settlement — California motorcycle, moderate injuries: Road rash, shoulder separation, 4 months treatment. Comparative negligence 20% on rider (lane position dispute). Settlement range $60,000–$95,000.

Comparable settlement — California pedestrian struck in crosswalk: Sacramento, broken pelvis, 2-week hospitalization. Clear liability. Settlement range $175,000–$250,000 including future care estimate.
